The Quiet Value of Owning Your Reading

When a service shuts down, the reading you saved does not have to disappear with it. Owning your archive means the articles you cared about stay yours, searchable and readable, for as long as you keep them.

A read-later tool should start from what you already have. Thousands of saved links are not clutter; they are a record of curiosity accumulated over years. The right home for them respects that history.

You should never be stranded by someone else's business decision.

Self-hosting turns a rented feature into a durable one. With clean text extraction and full-text search, an archive becomes a personal library rather than a graveyard of dead bookmarks.
